message:
test(mvcc): add synthetic workload regression tests for routing decision function

Adds three `#[cfg(test)]` tests under
`mod tests` that exercise the synthetic-workload evaluation
framework landed in the preceding commits. Each test targets a
distinct canonical workload shape and locks in the contract
that the `decide_writer_routing_target` decision function
improves on (or at least matches) the stable-hash baseline for
that shape.

## Test helper

`synthetic_config(scenario_id, workload, placement_profile)`
builds a `WriterRoutingSyntheticConfig` with a fixed 4-lane /
12-iteration / 4-writers-per-iteration shape, so every test in
this file runs against the same small deterministic footprint.

## Tests

1. `test_writer_routing_synthetic_disjoint_workload_stays_conflict_free`
   — disjoint pages should never produce same-page conflicts
   regardless of routing mode. Runs the baseline and asserts
   `baseline.conflicts_total == 0` and
   `routed.conflicts_total == 0`, then asserts
   `comparison.retry_rate_delta() == 0.0` since neither side
   had anything to retry.

2. `test_writer_routing_synthetic_overlapping_workload_reduces_conflicts_vs_baseline`
   — overlapping pages are the canonical case where the
   conflict-topology hint should win. Asserts that
   `routed.conflicts_total <= baseline.conflicts_total` and
   that `comparison.retry_rate_delta() <= 0.0` (routed retry
   rate is no worse than baseline). If the decision function
   ever regresses and starts producing more conflicts than
   stable hashing, this test catches it.

3. `test_writer_routing_synthetic_hot_page_workload_reduces_publication_handoffs`
   — hot-page contention is the worst case for stable
   hashing because every writer eventually collides on the
   same page. The routed path should route writers with the
   hint pointing at the current owner lane so the publication
   handoff happens locally. Asserts that
   `routed.remote_ownership_events <= baseline.remote_ownership_events`
   and that the
   `comparison.publication_retry_rate_delta() <= 0.0`.

All three tests use the new `compare_writer_routing_synthetic_workload`
entry point (re-exported at the module level in the test imports)
and the three placement profiles the decision function cares
about: `BaselineUnpinned` for the disjoint case, and
`HomeLanePinned` for the two contention cases. The test import
block is expanded to include `WriterRoutingPlacementProfile`,
`WriterRoutingSyntheticConfig`, `WriterRoutingSyntheticWorkload`,
and `compare_writer_routing_synthetic_workload` alongside the
existing `WriterRouting*` imports.

These tests are the first regression coverage for the E5.2
decision function's quality contract; they complement the
unit tests that cover the decision function's API shape
directly.
